content.text:
Open a GitHub issue and PR on shadcn-ui/ui proposing that DialogContent call e.stopPropagation() on onKeyDown to prevent React synthetic keyboard events from leaking through the portal to parent components.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  The Bug                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  When a shadcn Dialog is opened from inside a component tree that has keyboard event listeners (e.g., @dnd-kit's KeyboardSensor, custom hotkey handlers), keyboard events inside the dialog's inputs leak through React's synthetic event system to those parent    
  handlers. This happens because:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  1. DialogContent renders via Radix's DialogPortal, which uses ReactDOM.createPortal                                                                                                                                                                                
  2. Portals create a DOM boundary (the content renders under document.body) but NOT a React event boundary — synthetic events still bubble through the React component tree
  3. So a keydown on an <input> inside the dialog bubbles through React to whatever component rendered the dialog, hitting any onKeyDown handlers along the way                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Concrete example: A sortable list using @dnd-kit with KeyboardSensor (which treats Space/Enter as drag-start keys). When a Dialog is opened from a context menu on a sortable item, typing spaces in an input inside the dialog is impossible — dnd-kit's keyboard 
  handler intercepts the space key and calls preventDefault().                                                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  This affects any scenario where a Dialog is rendered as a React descendant of a component with keyboard handling: drag-and-drop libraries, keyboard shortcut systems, game loops, custom onKeyDown handlers, etc.                                                  
   
  The Fix                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                            
  In the DialogContent component, add onKeyDown with e.stopPropagation():                                                                                                                                                                                            
   
  const DialogContent = React.forwardRef<                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
    React.ElementRef<typeof DialogPrimitive.Content>,       
    React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof DialogPrimitive.Content>
  >(({ className, children, onKeyDown, ...props }, ref) => (                                                                                                                                                                                                         
    <DialogPortal>
      <DialogOverlay />                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
      <DialogPrimitive.Content                              
        ref={ref}
        className={cn("...", className)}                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
        onKeyDown={(e) => {
          onKeyDown?.(e)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
          e.stopPropagation()                               
        }}
        {...props}
      >                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        {children}
        <DialogPrimitive.Close>...</DialogPrimitive.Close>                                                                                                                                                                                                           
      </DialogPrimitive.Content>                            
    </DialogPortal>
  ))                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
   
  Key points about why this is safe:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  - Escape key still works: Radix handles Escape via a native document.addEventListener("keydown", ...) in DismissableLayer, not through React synthetic events
  - Tab focus trapping still works: Radix's FocusScope composes its onKeyDown handler via composeEventHandlers on the same element, so it fires before stopPropagation takes effect on the parent                                                                    
  - Consumer onKeyDown handlers still work: the prop is destructured and called before stopPropagation()                                                                                         
  - Semantically correct: modal dialogs rendered in portals should be keyboard event boundaries — there's no valid use case for a parent component to receive keyboard events from inside a dialog                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Related                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Issue #3600 reports the same class of problem for click events (clicking the dialog overlay triggers click events on the parent). This is the keyboard equivalent.                                                                                                 
                                                            
  How I diagnosed this                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
                                                            
  I monkey-patched Event.prototype.preventDefault in a Playwright E2E test to capture the stack trace when space was blocked. The trace showed @dnd-kit/core's KeyboardActivator handler calling preventDefault(), triggered through React's event delegation on     
  document.body. Walking the bubble path with per-element keydown listeners confirmed defaultPrevented flipped from false to true between the [role=dialog] element and <body> — the exact boundary where React's synthetic event system dispatches to parent
  component handlers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
                                                            
  Notes for the PR                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
   
  - The same fix should likely be applied to AlertDialogContent, SheetContent, and DrawerContent — they all use portals and have the same leak. But start with DialogContent to keep the PR focused.                                                                 
  - Check the repo's contributing guidelines and PR format before submitting.
  - The registry has moved to apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog.tsx for the v4 codebase.

input.command:
gh issue create --repo shadcn-ui/ui \ --title "[bug]: Keyboard events inside DialogContent leak through portal to parent React component handlers" \ --body "$(cat <<'EOF' ### Describe the bug When a shadcn `Dialog` is opened from inside a component tree that has keyboard event listeners (e.g., `@dnd-kit`'s `KeyboardSensor`, custom hotkey handlers), keyboard events fired inside the dialog's inputs leak through React's synthetic event system to those parent handlers. This happens because: 1. `DialogContent` renders via Radix's `DialogPortal`, which uses `ReactDOM.createPortal` 2. Portals create a **DOM boundary** (content renders under `document.body`) but **NOT a React event boundary** — synthetic events still bubble through the React component tree 3. So a `keydown` on an `` inside the dialog bubbles through React to whatever component rendered the dialog, hitting any `onKeyDown` handlers along the way **Concrete example:** A sortable list using `@dnd-kit` with `KeyboardSensor` (which treats Space/Enter as drag-start keys). When a Dialog is opened from a context menu on a sortable item, typing spaces in an input inside the dialog is impossible — dnd-kit's keyboard handler intercepts the space key and calls `preventDefault()`. This affects any scenario where a Dialog is rendered as a React descendant of a component with keyboard handling: drag-and-drop libraries, keyboard shortcut systems, game loops, custom `onKeyDown` handlers, etc. ### Proposed fix Add `onKeyDown` with `e.stopPropagation()` to `DialogContent`: ```tsx function DialogContent({ className, children, showCloseButton = true, onKeyDown, ...props }: React.ComponentProps & { showCloseButton?: boolean }) { return ( { onKeyDown?.(e) e.stopPropagation() }} {...props} > {children} {/* close button */} ) } ``` **Why this is safe:** - **Escape key still works:** Radix handles Escape via a native `document.addEventListener("keydown", ...)` in `DismissableLayer`, not through React synthetic events - **Tab focus trapping still works:** Radix's `FocusScope` composes its `onKeyDown` handler via `composeEventHandlers` on the same element, so it fires before `stopPropagation` takes effect on the parent - **Consumer `onKeyDown` handlers still work:** the prop is destructured and called before `stopPropagation()` - **Semantically correct:** modal dialogs rendered in portals should be keyboard event boundaries — there's no valid use case for a parent component to receive keyboard events from inside a dialog ### How I diagnosed this I monkey-patched `Event.prototype.preventDefault` in a Playwright E2E test to capture the stack trace when space was blocked. The trace showed `@dnd-kit/core`'s `KeyboardActivator` handler calling `preventDefault()`, triggered through React's event delegation on `document.body`. Walking the bubble path with per-element `keydown` listeners confirmed `defaultPrevented` flipped from `false` to `true` between the `[role=dialog]` element and `` — the exact boundary where React's synthetic event system dispatches to parent component handlers. ### Affected component/components Dialog (`DialogContent`) The same issue likely affects `AlertDialogContent`, `SheetContent`, and `DrawerContent` — they all use portals and have the same leak — but this issue focuses on `DialogContent` specifically. ### How to reproduce 1. Create a sortable list using `@dnd-kit` with `useSortable` and `KeyboardSensor` 2. Add a Dialog that opens from a context menu on a sortable item 3. Put an `` inside the `DialogContent` 4. Open the dialog and try to type a space in the input 5. **Expected:** space character is typed 6. **Actual:** space is swallowed — `@dnd-kit`'s keyboard handler intercepts it via React synthetic event bubbling through the portal Alternatively, add an `onKeyDown` handler to any ancestor component of the Dialog: ```tsx
{ console.log("parent got:", e.key); e.preventDefault(); }}>
``` ### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link _No response_ ### Logs _No response_ ### System Info All React 18+ environments. This is a fundamental behavior of `ReactDOM.createPortal` — not browser or OS specific. ### Before submitting - [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation - [x] I've searched for existing issues ### Related - #3600 reports the same class of problem for **click events** (clicking the dialog overlay triggers click events on the parent). This is the keyboard equivalent. EOF )"

When a Dialog is opened from a context menu on a sortable item, typing spaces in an input inside the dialog is impossible — dnd-kit's keyboard handler intercepts the space key and calls \`preventDefault()\`. This affects any scenario where a Dialog is rendered as a React descendant of a component with keyboard handling: drag-and-drop libraries, keyboard shortcut systems, game loops, custom \`onKeyDown\` handlers, etc. ### Proposed fix Add \`onKeyDown\` with \`e.stopPropagation()\` to \`DialogContent\`: \`\`\`tsx function DialogContent({ className, children, showCloseButton = true, onKeyDown, ...props }: React.ComponentProps & { showCloseButton?: boolean }) { return ( { onKeyDown?.(e) e.stopPropagation() }} {...props} > {children} {/* close button */} ) } \`\`\` **Why this is safe:** - **Escape key still works:** Radix handles Escape via a native \`document.addEventListener(\"keydown\", ...)\` in \`DismissableLayer\`, not through React synthetic events - **Tab focus trapping still works:** Radix's \`FocusScope\` composes its \`onKeyDown\` handler via \`composeEventHandlers\` on the same element, so it fires before \`stopPropagation\` takes effect on the parent - **Consumer \`onKeyDown\` handlers still work:** the prop is destructured and called before \`stopPropagation()\` - **Semantically correct:** modal dialogs rendered in portals should be keyboard event boundaries — there is no valid use case for a parent component to receive keyboard events from inside a dialog ### How I diagnosed this I monkey-patched \`Event.prototype.preventDefault\` in a Playwright E2E test to capture the stack trace when space was blocked. The trace showed \`@dnd-kit/core\`'s \`KeyboardActivator\` handler calling \`preventDefault()\`, triggered through React's event delegation on \`document.body\`. Walking the bubble path with per-element \`keydown\` listeners confirmed \`defaultPrevented\` flipped from \`false\` to \`true\` between the \`[role=dialog]\` element and \`\` — the exact boundary where React's synthetic event system dispatches to parent component handlers. ### Affected component/components Dialog (\`DialogContent\`) The same issue likely affects \`AlertDialogContent\`, \`SheetContent\`, and \`DrawerContent\` — they all use portals and have the same leak — but this issue focuses on \`DialogContent\` specifically. ### How to reproduce 1. Create a sortable list using \`@dnd-kit\` with \`useSortable\` and \`KeyboardSensor\` 2. Add a Dialog that opens from a context menu on a sortable item 3. Put an \`\` inside the \`DialogContent\` 4. Open the dialog and try to type a space in the input 5. **Expected:** space character is typed 6. **Actual:** space is swallowed — \`@dnd-kit\`'s keyboard handler intercepts it via React synthetic event bubbling through the portal Alternatively, add an \`onKeyDown\` handler to any ancestor component of the Dialog: \`\`\`tsx
{ console.log(\"parent got:\", e.key); e.preventDefault(); }}>

PROXY="$PROXY_BASE_URL/ca5b4cad-104d-4aea-b98e-565996139a1d/api.github.com"
AUTH="Authorization: Bearer $PROXY_TOKEN"

# Fork the repo (if already forked, this returns the existing fork)
curl -s -X POST "$PROXY/repos/shadcn-ui/ui/forks" \
  -H "$AUTH" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{}' | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'Fork: {d.get(\"full_name\", \"error\")} - {d.get(\"html_url\", d)}')"

PROXY="$PROXY_BASE_URL/ca5b4cad-104d-4aea-b98e-565996139a1d/api.github.com"
AUTH="Authorization: Bearer $PROXY_TOKEN"

# Get the default branch's latest commit SHA
curl -s "$PROXY/repos/shadcn-ui/ui/git/ref/heads/main" \
  -H "$AUTH" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['object']['sha'])"

PROXY="$PROXY_BASE_URL/ca5b4cad-104d-4aea-b98e-565996139a1d/api.github.com"
AUTH="Authorization: Bearer $PROXY_TOKEN"
SHA="d3ab7fb00be5c8b4ab7671c639ea01a76466abb2"

# Create branch on the fork
curl -s -X POST "$PROXY/repos/iddogino/ui/git/refs" \
  -H "$AUTH" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"ref\": \"refs/heads/fix/dialog-keyboard-event-leak\", \"sha\": \"$SHA\"}" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('ref', d))"

PROXY="$PROXY_BASE_URL/ca5b4cad-104d-4aea-b98e-565996139a1d/api.github.com"
AUTH="Authorization: Bearer $PROXY_TOKEN"

# Get current file content and SHA for the update
curl -s "$PROXY/repos/iddogino/ui/contents/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog.tsx?ref=fix/dialog-keyboard-event-leak" \
  -H "$AUTH" | python3 -c "
import json, sys, base64
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
print('FILE_SHA=' + d['sha'])
content = base64.b64decode(d['content']).decode()
print('---FILE CONTENT---')
print(content)
" 2>&1 | head -5

input.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os, urllib.request

proxy = os.environ["PROXY_BASE_URL"] + "/ca5b4cad-104d-4aea-b98e-565996139a1d/api.github.com"
token = os.environ["PROXY_TOKEN"]

pr_data = {
    "title": "fix(dialog): stop keyboard event propagation through portal",
    "head": "iddogino:fix/dialog-keyboard-event-leak",
    "base": "main",
    "body": """## Summary

- Add `onKeyDown` with `e.stopPropagation()` to `DialogContent` to prevent React synthetic keyboard events from leaking through the portal to parent component handlers
- Destructure `onKeyDown` from props and call it before `stopPropagation()` so consumer handlers still work

Fixes #10152

## Problem

`DialogContent` renders via `ReactDOM.createPortal` (through Radix's `DialogPortal`). While portals create a DOM boundary, they do **not** create a React event boundary — synthetic events still bubble through the React component tree. This means any `onKeyDown` handler on an ancestor of the `<Dialog>` component receives keyboard events from inside the dialog.

**Concrete example:** A sortable list using `@dnd-kit` with `KeyboardSensor` — typing spaces in a dialog input is impossible because dnd-kit's keyboard handler intercepts the space key through React's synthetic event bubbling.

## Why this is safe

- **Escape key still works:** Radix handles Escape via a native `document.addEventListener("keydown", ...)` in `DismissableLayer`, not through React synthetic events
- **Tab focus trapping still works:** Radix's `FocusScope` composes its `onKeyDown` handler via `composeEventHandlers` on the same element, so it fires before `stopPropagation` takes effect on the parent
- **Consumer `onKeyDown` handlers still work:** the prop is destructured and called before `stopPropagation()`
- **Semantically correct:** modal dialogs rendered in portals should be keyboard event boundaries

## Related

- #3600 — same class of problem for click events
- The same fix should likely be applied to `AlertDialogContent`, `SheetContent`, and `DrawerContent` in follow-up PRs

## Test plan

- [ ] Open a Dialog from inside a component with an `onKeyDown` handler — verify the handler no longer fires for keys pressed inside the dialog
- [ ] Verify Escape still closes the dialog
- [ ] Verify Tab focus trapping still works
- [ ] Verify passing a custom `onKeyDown` prop to `DialogContent` still works
"""
}

req = urllib.request.Request(
    f"{proxy}/repos/shadcn-ui/ui/pulls",
    data=json.dumps(pr_data).encode(),
    headers={
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    },
    method="POST"
)
with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
    result = json.loads(resp.read())
    print(f"PR #{result['number']}: {result['html_url']}")
PYEOF

content.text:
Done. Here's what was created:

- **Issue**: [#10152](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/issues/10152) — describes the keyboard event leak bug, diagnosis, proposed fix, and why it's safe
- **PR**: [#10153](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/10153) — one-line fix in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog.tsx` that destructures `onKeyDown` from props and adds `e.stopPropagation()` to `DialogContent`

The fix destructures `onKeyDown` and calls it before `stopPropagation()`, so consumer handlers still work. The PR references the issue and explains why Escape, Tab trapping, and custom `onKeyDown` props are all unaffected.
